{"step_by_step_thinking": "The solvent-detergent (SD) method disrupts the membranes of lipid-enveloped viruses. HIV, HBV, and HCV are all lipid-enveloped viruses, while HAV is not. Therefore, SD plasma would destroy HIV, HBV, and HCV, but not HAV. Given the options, the likely possibility of infection after SD plasma transfusion is HIV.", "answer_choice": "A"}
